subsections.title: Highlight Cards
content: - Lower error in long-horizon forecasting: MAE is reduced by 5.2% and 3.6% versus iTransformer and PatchTST respectively
- Zero-shot transfer cost cut in half: Covers data-scarce scenarios without full-scale training
- Validation demo completed in 3-5 days: Compresses the validation cycle from 2-3 weeks to within 1 week
- More trustworthy solution selection: Automated benchmarking allows non-algorithm practitioners to judge strengths and weaknesses quickly

subsections.title: 2.1 Overview
content: **Business Background**: Long-horizon time-series forecasting is widely used in scenarios such as monitoring and early warning for various feature indicators in fields like energy, helping people detect risks and anomalies in time based on forecast trends and then make adjustments.

**Solution**: This example targets smart energy scenarios such as electricity, water, and natural gas. It takes historical sequence data of specified features in the scenario as input and outputs predicted values for multiple future time points over a long period. By introducing the latest time-series models from 2025, it captures long-cycle abnormal states and provides trustworthy decision support for energy scheduling and control.

subsections.title: 2.2 Classification Tags
content: **Industry**: Energy & Manufacturing
**Category**: Operations, Risk Control
**Technology Category**: Statistical Machine Learning, Large Language Model (LLM), Operations (Testing & Monitoring), Time-Series Forecasting

subsections.title: 2.3 Implementation Cycle
content: - Demo development: 3-5 days

subsections.title: 2.4 Team Composition
content: - Demo phase: 1 business expert, 1 algorithm engineer

subsections.title: 2.5 Business Pain Points
content: - Manual forecasting is costly and error-prone: Manual forecasting depends on practitioner experience, senior experts are expensive, and multiple types of indicators cannot be continuously tracked and predicted at the same time; ordinary staff have limited judgment capability and large errors

- Poor stability in long-horizon forecasting: Existing models show significantly amplified error when forecasting 720+ future time points as the horizon increases, making it difficult to capture key periodic patterns and sudden anomalies and causing warning failures

- Low delivery credibility: Existing technical approaches lack a unified automated benchmarking process, and different organizations produce different comparison metrics and results, creating confusion in technology selection and best practices

subsections.title: 2.6 Core Functions
content: - Zero-shot long-range forecasting: Stably outputs predicted values for 720+ future time points on unseen new business data
- Intelligent ranking for solution selection: Automatically compares specified mainstream time-series forecasting models, generates ranking results, and provides a basis for selection

subsections.title: 3.1.1 Reasons for Best Practice Selection
content: - More accurate forecasting: On the industry's core benchmark, MAE is 38.4%, outperforming the mainstream SOTA methods from the previous year.
- Lighter transfer: Zero-shot performance is close to fully trained solutions, reducing business-side transfer cost by about 50%.
- Faster decisions: Automated benchmarking helps complete solution selection quickly and significantly improves trustworthiness.
- More efficient implementation: Under a low-code framework, a demo can be completed in 3-5 days, shortening the validation cycle to within 1 week.

subsections.title: 3.1.4 Metrics
content: **Core Business Metrics**

- Solution selection credibility improves by 10% compared with algorithm researchers and by up to 70% compared with non-algorithm practitioners, enabling business staff to make choices quickly and accurately
- Solution selection and transfer cost reduced by 50%
- Mean Absolute Error (MAE) < 0.39, reducing losses caused by long-horizon forecasting errors in the energy sector by about 5%
**Core Technical Metrics**
- Mean Absolute Error (MAE) < 0.39: Same as the core business metric
